message:
fix(web): tighten copy across core-flywheel visualizations for concision

- artifact-ladder: shorten heading to "What you produce at each stage"
- human-agent-timeline: compress AGENTS.md stage description, simplify
  monitoring insight ("About 5 minutes..."), use second-person voice
- quick-nav: rename "The Feeling" to "In Practice"
- swarm-chaos: trim em-dashes, condense final comparison prose, shorten
  intro paragraph

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -598,7 +598,7 @@ export function ArtifactLadderViz() { Artifact Progression

- How artifacts transform through the core loop + What you produce at each stage

An idea becomes a plan, a plan becomes beads, beads become

@@ -78,9 +78,9 @@ const STAGES: TimelineStage[] = [ color: HUMAN_COLOR, icon: BookOpen, who: "You", - what: "Create the operating manual that tells agents how to behave, what tools to use, and what coordination rules to follow.", + what: "Write the operating manual: agent behavior rules, tool usage, and coordination protocol.", tools: "Your text editor", - insight: "This one file is what makes everything else automatic.", + insight: "Every agent reads this file on startup. It replaces verbal instructions.", }, { id: "launch", @@ -127,7 +127,7 @@ const STAGES: TimelineStage[] = [ what: "Check bv for stuck beads, rescue confused agents by telling them to reread AGENTS.md, add missing beads.", tools: "bv --robot-triage, Agent Mail inbox", insight: - "This is light maintenance, not micromanagement. 5 minutes every 15.", + "About 5 minutes of checking every 15 minutes.", }, { id: "review", @@ -683,9 +683,9 @@ export function HumanAgentTimelineViz() { Who Does What in the Core Loop

- The human designs the system and tends the swarm.{" "} + You design the system and tend the swarm.{" "} - The agents handle all the coordination plumbing. + Agents do the coordination work.
